channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: American who says he crossed into Syria by foot is free after 7 months in detention
item.description: DAMASCUS, Syria (AP) — An American who turned up in Syria on Thursday says he was detained after crossing into the country by foot on a Christian pilgrimage seven months ago.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump invites China's Xi to his inauguration even as he threatens massive tariffs on Beijing
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has invited Chinese President Xi Jinping to attend his inauguration next month.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump is named Time's Person of the Year, will ring the New York Stock Exchange bell
item.description: About six months ago, Donald Trump was sitting in a courtroom in lower Manhattan listening to a jury make him the first former president convicted of a crime.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE and DAVID BAUDER Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: North Dakota regulators consider underground carbon dioxide storage permits for Midwest pipeline
item.description: BISMARCK, N.D. (AP) — A North Dakota panel will consider Thursday whether to approve permits for underground storage of hundreds of millions of metric tons of carbon dioxide that a proposed pipeline would carry from ethanol plants throughout the Midwest.
item.dc:creator: JACK DURA and STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: FIFA confirms Saudi Arabia as 2034 World Cup host despite human rights concerns
item.description: ZURICH (AP) — Saudi Arabia was officially confirmed Wednesday by FIFA as host of the 2034 World Cup in men’s soccer, giving the oil-rich kingdom its biggest prize yet for massive spending on global sports driven by Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM DUNBAR, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Aftermath of small plane crash onto Texas roadway caught on camera with split fuselage, damaged vehicles
item.description: The FAA and the Victoria Police Department in Texas said a small plane crashed into a busy intersection, colliding with three vehicles Wednesday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden says healthy women help US prosperity as he highlights White House initiative on their health
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden said Wednesday that he is “so proud” that a women's health research initiative he launched last year at his wife's urging has already invested nearly $1 billion because a healthy female population improves U.S. prosperity.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Connecticut man celebrates birthday by giving back to the community
item.description: "We actually just celebrated my milestone 50th birthday last week with a very well-attended cookout," said Rippy Patton who organized the celebration.
item.dc:creator: Alyssa Taglia, NewsNation

item.title: Bernie Sanders says this will likely be his last term in Senate
item.description: Sen. Bernie Sanders has served in Congress since 1991, spending 16 years in the House before being elected to the Senate in 2006.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Trump, team trail Fauci on trust as medical information source: Survey
item.description: Trump and other Republicans have criticized Fauci, who was the face of the first Trump and Biden administration’s response against COVID-19, over his handling of the pandemic.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KRON4

item.title: The burial site of the people Andrew Jackson enslaved was lost. The Hermitage says it is found
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — At least 26 enslaved people died on the Tennessee plantation of President Andrew Jackson between 1804 and the end of the Civil War in 1865. Where they were laid to rest is knowledge that had been lost to time.
item.dc:creator: TRAVIS LOLLER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Kayaker who faked his death, fled the US charged after returning to Wisconsin
item.description: Police had previously said they believed Borgwardt planned his Aug. 12 disappearance and tried to conceal his efforts to mislead investigations.
item.dc:creator: Indiana Schilz, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: GOP senators say they won’t fight Trump on Jan. 6 pardons
item.description: Republican senators who witnessed firsthand the Jan. 6, 2021, ransacking of congressional offices now say it’s up to President-elect Donald Trump whether to pardon those protesters convicted of federal crimes.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Sanders says this will likely be his last term
item.description: Sen. Bernie Sanders (I-Vt.), one of the Senate’s most prominent progressives, says he likely won’t run again for the Senate after his fourth term expires in 2030.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KRON4

item.title: How to pick the perfect Windows laptop
item.description: Shopping for a new computer can be intimidating. There are so many models to choose from and so many specs to decide on. "They get really excited when they come in here.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KTLA 5

item.title: Luigi Mangione was reported missing 2 weeks before CEO slain: police
item.description: The parents of Luigi Mangione, the man suspected of shooting and killing UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, told law enforcement that they hadn't heard from their son in months.
item.dc:creator: Video credit: Nicole Johnson, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Friends, acquaintances defend Mangione family as media descends on alleged CEO killer's hometown
item.description: Alleged CEO killer Luigi Mangione's family has a deep footprint in the state of Maryland, and friends and acquaintances of the Mangiones say they were shocked at the news of Luigi's arrest.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A Malibu wildfire prompts evacuation orders and warnings for 20,000, including Dick Van Dyke, Cher
item.description: Weather conditions were forecast to improve this week in Southern California, aiding firefighters in their battle against a wind-driven wildfire that's forced up to 20,000 people — including performers Cher, Dick Van Dyke and his wife — from their homes.
item.dc:creator: JAE C. HONG, ERIC THAYER and STEFANIE DAZIO Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Only about 2 in 10 Americans approve of Biden's pardon of his son Hunter, an AP-NORC poll finds
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Only about 2 in 10 Americans approve of President Joe Biden’s decision to pardon his son Hunter after earlier promising he would do no such thing, according to a poll from The Associated Press-NORC Center for Public Affairs Research.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and AMELIA THOMSON-DEVEAUX, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Florida man kills father, wounds mother after father told him to stop playing video games, get a job
item.description: A Florida man allegedly shot and killed his father and wounded his mother after his father nagged him for spending too much time playing video games and not getting a job.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Oregon federal judge temporarily blocks proposed Kroger-Albertsons merger
item.description: Oregon was one of several states to join the Federal Trade Commission’s lawsuit that aimed to block the merger.
item.dc:creator: Jashayla Pettigrew, KTLA 5

item.title: San Diego sheriff defies new policy to limit cooperation with immigration officials
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — The sheriff of the nation’s fifth-largest county on Tuesday defied a new policy to limit cooperation with federal immigration authorities, setting up a showdown over a new obstacle to President-elect Donald Trump’s mass deportation plans.
item.dc:creator: ELLIOT SPAGAT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Celebrities and coastal residents flee from wind-driven wildfire in Malibu
item.description: MALIBU, Calif. (AP) — Evacuation orders and warnings have gone out to 20,000 Southern California residents Tuesday as firefighters battled a wind-driven wildfire in Malibu that burned near celebrities' seaside mansions, horse farms and Pepperdine University, the sheriff's department said.
item.dc:creator: JAE C. HONG, ERIC THAYER and CHRISTOPHER WEBER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: After acquittal in subway chokehold trial, Daniel Penny says he was vulnerable in the encounter
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — After being acquitted of homicide, the military veteran who choked a volatile, mentally ill man on a New York subway told an interviewer he put himself in a “very vulnerable position” but felt compelled to act.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ and SANYA MANSOOR, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Joe Burrow's home broken into during Monday Night Football
item.description: Bengals quarterback Joe Burrow's home was broken into during Monday Night Football in the latest home invasion of a pro athlete in the U.S., authorities said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: McDonald's in Pennsylvania slammed with bad reviews after arrest of Luigi Mangione
item.description: A McDonald's in Altoona, Pennsylvania, is getting backlash online after an employee called 911, leading to the arrest of Luigi Mangione, the suspect in the killing of United Healthcare CEO Brian Thompson.
item.dc:creator: Bill Shannon, KTLA 5

item.title: When fire threatened a California university, the school says it knew what to do
item.description: MALIBU, Calif. (AP) — The wildfire alert came in the middle of the night as some college students in Southern California were cramming for final exams and others were woken up in their dorms.
item.dc:creator: AMY TAXIN and ERIC THAYER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Mangione family in Maryland: What we know
item.description: Although many people outside of Maryland came to know the "Mangione" name only after the arrest of Luigi Mangione, 26, who is accused of killing UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, the family has a number of prominent ties to the state where he grew up.
item.dc:creator: Brian Farrell, KRON4

item.title: From wealth and success to murder suspect, the life of Luigi Mangione took a hard turn
item.description: Luigi Nicholas Mangione, the suspect in the fatal shooting of a healthcare executive in New York City, apparently was living a charmed life: the grandson of a wealthy real estate developer, valedictorian of his elite Baltimore prep school and with degrees from one of the nation's top private univers
item.dc:creator: SEAN MURPHY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US wildlife officials plan to label monarch butterflies as threatened species
item.description: The U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service plans to add the butterfly to the threatened species list by the end of next year following an extensive public comment period.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Santa’s home is on Zillow, and you can take an AI-powered tour
item.description: You can take an AI-powered tour of Santa’s home, and you can even decorate your home like Santa with these festive décor ideas.
item.dc:creator: Christina Marfice, KTLA 5

item.title: Las Vegas judge dismisses infamous 'Black Widow' murder case after 27 years
item.description: The judge dismissed murder charges against Margaret Rudin, the woman dubbed "The Black Widow" who a jury convicted of killing her husband more than two decades ago.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KRON4

item.title: What we know about Luigi Mangione, the suspect arrested in UnitedHealthcare CEO killing
item.description: A man arrested in connection with the fatal shooting of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson has been identified as Luigi Mangione, officials said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Cook, KTLA 5

item.title: How to watch 'Monday Night Football' in 'The Simpsons' universe
item.description: The prime-time matchup between the Cincinnati Bengals and Dallas Cowboys will also take place at Springfield’s Atoms Stadium as part of “The Simpsons Funday Football” alternate broadcast.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: La Linea, Sinaloa cartel unleash violence in Chihuahua
item.description: Mexican authorities say they have “restored order” in parts of the state of Chihuahua where organized criminal gangs engaged in violent confrontations in the past few days.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, KTLA 5

item.title: Oldest known stone tablet inscribed with Ten Commandments to be auctioned
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The oldest known stone tablet inscribed with the Ten Commandments, dating from 300 to 800 A.D., will be sold in New York this month, Sotheby's auction house said. The 155-pound (52-kilogram) marble slab inscribed with the commandments in Paleo-Hebrew script will be auctioned on Dec.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Suspect in UnitedHealthcare CEO killing charged with murder, court records show
item.description: A man in Pennsylvania has been arrested in connection with the fatal shooting of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, officials said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, WFLA, NBC
